activities.title: Reading Comprehension through Unseen Passage
activities.details: Students engage in reading discursive passages (400-450 words) and case-based factual passages with visual input (200-250 words). Activities include answering Multiple Choice Questions, Objective Type Questions, and Very Short Answer Questions to assess comprehension, interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, and vocabulary.

activities.title: Grammar Accuracy Exercises
activities.details: Students practice Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, and Reported speech through Gap Filling, Editing, and Transformation exercises. Focus is on accurate use of spelling, punctuation, and grammar.

activities.title: Descriptive Paragraph Writing
activities.details: Students write descriptive paragraphs (100-120 words) describing a person, event, or situation based on visual or verbal cues.

activities.title: Story Writing and Diary Entry
activities.details: Students write a story on a given cue/title or a diary entry (100-120 words) to demonstrate creative expression.

activities.title: Reference to the Context Analysis
activities.details: Students analyze extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry, answering Multiple Choice Questions to assess interpretation, analysis, inference, evaluation, appreciation, and vocabulary.

activities.title: Short Answer Questions Discussion
activities.details: Students answer short answer questions (40-50 words) from Beehive and Moments to assess interpretation, analysis, inference, and evaluation.

activities.title: Long Answer Type Creative Writing
activities.details: Students answer long answer questions (100-120 words) from Beehive and Moments, focusing on creativity, imagination, extrapolation beyond the text, theme interpretation, or character sketches.

activities.title: Role Play and Skit Performance
activities.details: Students participate in role plays, skits, and dramatizations based on texts from Beehive and Moments to enhance listening and speaking skills.

activities.title: Group Discussion and Peer Interaction
activities.details: Students engage in classroom interactions, group work, and discussions to express ideas, defend views, and participate actively.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Project: Role Play/Skit/Dramatization
projects.details: Students create and perform art-integrated projects based on activities like role play, skit, or dramatization derived from the prescribed texts (Beehive/Moments) to assess listening and speaking skills.

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Skills Assessment
practicals.details: Regular practice and assessment of listening and speaking skills (05 marks) involving oral activities, discussions, and presentations based on the curriculum texts.
